title: Avoid ActiveRecord N+1 with eager loading
description: Eager-load associations with includes/preload so a collection issues a bounded number of queries.
body: Use when rendering or iterating a collection that touches an association per row. Do not access record.association inside a loop without preloading. Actions: add .includes(:assoc) (or preload/eager_load) so Rails batches the association into one or two queries, select only needed columns, and use find_each to batch large result sets. Validation signals include a request/model spec asserting a constant query count regardless of row count, often via a query-count matcher or the log. Risk boundary: an unbounded N+1 scales linearly with data and overloads the database under production load; confirm the query count dropped, not merely that the view renders.
